summary: Product owner explicitly rejects the current map drawing direction and spatial indexing direction at the root level. The recovery must not treat the current topology-backed polygon map as a strategy-game terrain foundation that only needs visual polish.

decisions.decision: The current player-facing strategic terrain drawing direction is rejected at the root level; downstream work must replace it rather than polish it.
The current spatial indexing and interaction direction is rejected at the root level; downstream work must define a new authoritative terrain-route-node query model.
ADR-010 remains useful as anti-hidden-grid proof, but ADR-011 must supersede it for player-facing strategic terrain and spatial interaction.

clarifications.decision: The current result is not accepted as a low-fidelity strategic terrain map. It does not resemble the terrain readability expected from mature grand-strategy references.

The current terrain drawing approach is rejected. Replacing rectangular tiles with irregular-looking polygon shapes is not enough if the map still reads as abstract cells instead of terrain, routes, barriers, nodes, and strategic places.

The current spatial indexing and interaction direction is rejected. The next plan must define a new authoritative strategic terrain surface and hit-test/pathing model instead of preserving polygon-first governance footprints as the main spatial substrate.

ADR-010 remains useful as anti-hidden-grid proof, but it is not an acceptable target architecture for content lock. ADR-011 must supersede it for player-facing strategic terrain and spatial interaction.

required_next_plan_properties: Start from mature strategy-game map practice: continuous terrain readability, named strategic places, roads/river/coast/pass corridors, chokepoints, and route capacity/risk, not from a district-cell board.
Separate governance boundaries from terrain patches, barriers/channels, strategic nodes, route corridors, and player action targets.
Define hit testing and spatial queries around actionable terrain/route/node layers first, with governance districts as an overlay, not the core map substrate.
Require validation evidence that the map no longer behaves like a disguised rectangular/grid/lattice/cell board.
Require player-facing route and terrain reasons to come from the same query model used by AI and previews.

risks.summary: If the rejected drawing and spatial-index direction is preserved, M7 will harden a map that does not read as a mature strategic terrain game.
risks.mitigation: Require ADR-011 and downstream recovery tasks to replace the player-facing terrain/spatial model before content-lock reconsideration.

source_user_statement: 你也没做得像战略游戏地形。我对你目前地图绘制的方案、地图空间索引方案，从根本上就是彻底否定的态度
requested_action: Interrupt or amend the active M7-STRATEGIC-TERRAIN-RECOVERY-001 planning writer so ADR-011 and downstream tasks replace the rejected map/spatial-index direction rather than polishing it.
